name: GRANULOCYTIC SARCOMA
definition: CHLOROMA =  MYELOID SARCOMA = MYELOBLASTOMA uncommon extramedullary solid tumor consisting of primitive precursors of the granulocytic

demographics.Associated with: chronic myeloid leukemia, myelodysplastic syndrome, essential

dominant_finding: homogeneous enhancement on CT / MR (DDx to hematoma / abscess)
discriminating_features: soft-tissue mass infrequently with bone erosion / demineralization / periosteal reaction
generally homogeneously isoattenuating to slightly hyperattenuating relative to muscle /
homogeneous enhancement on CT / MR (DDx to hematoma / abscess)
iso- to hypointense relative to gray matter / bone marrow / muscle on T1WI
heterogeneously iso- to slightly hyperintense on T2WI

CT: soft-tissue mass infrequently with bone erosion / demineralization / periosteal reaction
generally homogeneously isoattenuating to slightly hyperattenuating relative to muscle /
homogeneous enhancement on CT / MR (DDx to hematoma / abscess)
MR: iso- to hypointense relative to gray matter / bone marrow / muscle on T1WI
heterogeneously iso- to slightly hyperintense on T2WI

clinical.Prognosis: poor outcome; resolution under chemotherapy ± radiation therapy; recurrence rate
